summary: Russian forces are using log-periodic directional antennas on "Molniyas" to counter FPV drone strikes, with advantages including wider operating range, increased range, resistance to video jamming, and use of vertical/horizontal polarization instead of circular polarization. Ukrainian FPV drones struck the Afipsky oil refinery in Krasnodar Krai on March 14, 2026, causing a massive fire detected by NASA's FIRMS monitoring service. Russian forces are actively targeting Ukrainian reconnaissance drones and fixed-wing UAVs like the "Hornet" using systems such as "Elka" and "Yolka," while also employing FPV drones to eliminate Ukrainian artillery positions and personnel. Ukrainian forces are using E-300 drones, rebuilt from light aircraft, in the Zaporizhzhia direction, and FPV pilots are employing tactics such as destabilizing enemy positions before strikes. A Russian soldier was killed in a bayonet charge against a Ukrainian FPV drone, and another was hit by two simultaneous FPV strikes while hiding behind a tree.
